title: Saint-Basile-le-Grand, Quebec

infobox.value: [J3N](/wiki/List_of_J_postal_codes_of_Canada "List of J postal codes of Canada")
This section **needs expansion**. You can help by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Saint-Basile-le-Grand&action=edit&section=). *(November 2014)*

(French pronunciation: [[sɛ̃ bazil lə ɡʁɑ̃]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French")) is a city located in [La Vallée-du-Richelieu Regional County Municipality](/wiki/La_Vall%C3%A9e-du-Richelieu_Regional_County_Municipality "La Vallée-du-Richelieu Regional County Municipality") in southwestern [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), Canada. The population as of the [2021 Canadian Census](/wiki/2021_Canadian_Census "2021 Canadian Census") was 17,053.

["*Banque de noms de lieux du Québec*: Reference number 55722"](https://toponymie.gouv.qc.ca/ct/ToposWeb/fiche.aspx?no_seq=55722). *toponymie.gouv.qc.ca* (in French). [Commission de toponymie du Québec](/wiki/Commission_de_toponymie_du_Qu%C3%A9bec "Commission de toponymie du Québec").

**Saint-Basile-le-Grand** (French pronunciation: [[sɛ̃ bazil lə ɡʁɑ̃]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French")) is a city located in [La Vallée-du-Richelieu Regional County Municipality](/wiki/La_Vall%C3%A9e-du-Richelieu_Regional_County_Municipality "La Vallée-du-Richelieu Regional County Municipality") in southwestern [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), Canada. The population as of the [2021 Canadian Census](/wiki/2021_Canadian_Census "2021 Canadian Census") was 17,053.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint-Basile-le-Grand&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

On June 15, 1871, Saint-Basile-le-Grand was created from territories coming from [Saint-Bruno-de-Montarville](/wiki/Saint-Bruno-de-Montarville "Saint-Bruno-de-Montarville") and [Saint-Joseph-de-Chambly](/wiki/Carignan,_Quebec "Carignan, Quebec"). On June 7, 1969, the municipality obtained its status as a city. Its territory, the majority of which is still agricultural, occupies an area of 35.84 km2 between the [Richelieu River](/wiki/Richelieu_River "Richelieu River") and [Mont Saint-Bruno](/wiki/Mont_Saint-Bruno "Mont Saint-Bruno").

On August 23, 1988, the city suffered an [arson attack](/wiki/Arson "Arson") on a [PCBs](/wiki/Polychlorinated_biphenyl "Polychlorinated biphenyl") [warehouse](/wiki/Warehouse "Warehouse").

For several years now, the citizens of Saint-Basile-le-Grand are able to take advantage of the commuter train. The station located at the corner of boulevard du Millénaire and boulevard Sir-Wilfrid-Laurier ([Route 116](/wiki/Quebec_Route_116 "Quebec Route 116")) also led, in 2004, to the construction of a small shopping center called "Place de la gare".

### The Church

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint-Basile-le-Grand&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: The Church")]

The church was finished at the beginning of January 1877, after a little less than a year of work. The church was built on land donated by Basile Daigneault.

Here is a quote from Mr. Moreau who went to the stone house of Basile Daigneault to hold his meeting:

> "Built [the house] on the land with Mr. Basile Daigneault as a neighbor on both sides, on Rang des Vingt-Quatre, /…/, it was found that the majority of the freeholder inhabitants wanted to buy it. Consequently, the meeting, considering that Mr. Basile Daigneault, owner of the land on which the said house is located, land which he offered free of charge to the deputy of Monsignor of Montreal to build the church, presbytery and other dependencies of the said parish , without including, however, the said stone house which is built there, asks to sell the said house, before making the contracts of the said land, so that, later, it will serve as a presbytery. ».

### The "Bunker"

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint-Basile-le-Grand&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: The \"Bunker\"")]

Saint-Basile-le-Grand also has its place of choice in the history of [organized crime](/wiki/Organized_crime "Organized crime"). Indeed, a fortified square was erected there in the 1990s, at 28 rue Alvarès. The property belonged to a satellite club of the [Hells Angels](/wiki/Hells_Angels "Hells Angels"), the Evil Ones. This was in the sights of the municipality in the early 2000s, when a regulation prohibiting fortifications (bulletproof glass, maximum exterior cameras and limitation of the height of fences) was adopted by the city council.

The bunker was seized during the roundup of Operation South in February 2004 to finally be destroyed in January 2006.

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint-Basile-le-Grand&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Demographics")]

In the [2021 Census of Population](/wiki/2021_Canadian_census "2021 Canadian census") conducted by [Statistics Canada](/wiki/Statistics_Canada "Statistics Canada"), Saint-Basile-le-Grand had a population of 17,053 living in 6,561 of its 6,635 total private dwellings, a change of -0% from its 2016 population of 17,059. With a land area of 35.84 km2 (13.84 sq mi), it had a population density of 475.8/km2 (1,232.3/sq mi) in 2021.[[5]](#cite_note-2021census-5)

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint-Basile-le-Grand&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Economy")]

Northvolt Six, a giga lithium-ion battery plant, is planned to be built in Saint-Basile-le-Grand. The plant will have an annual cell production capacity of 60 GWh, with adjacent facilities for the production of active cathode and recycling materials. This will be Northvolt's first plant outside Europe. Construction of the first 30 GWh phase of the project is expected to begin by the end of 2023 and the first batteries are expected to be delivered in 2026.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Infrastructure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint-Basile-le-Grand&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Infrastructure")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/26/AMTSaintBasile.JPG/250px-AMTSaintBasile.JPG)](/wiki/File:AMTSaintBasile.JPG)

Train station in Saint-Basile.

Saint-Basile-le-Grand is served by the [Saint-Basile-le-Grand](/wiki/Saint-Basile-le-Grand_station "Saint-Basile-le-Grand station"), a [commuter rail](/wiki/Commuter_rail "Commuter rail") station on the [Exo](/wiki/Exo_(public_transit) "Exo (public transit)")'s [Mont-Saint-Hilaire line](/wiki/Mont-Saint-Hilaire_line "Mont-Saint-Hilaire line"). Local bus service is provided by the [Exo de la Vallée du Richelieu sector](/wiki/Exo_de_la_Vall%C3%A9e_du_Richelieu_sector "Exo de la Vallée du Richelieu sector").

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint-Basile-le-Grand&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Education")]

The [South Shore Protestant Regional School Board](/wiki/South_Shore_Protestant_Regional_School_Board "South Shore Protestant Regional School Board") previously served the municipality.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
